# Neuman Brain - Post-P0-P3 Era

## Current State (Updated: 2025-11-30)

**Shipped:** v1.0 Foundation (2025-11-30)
**Status:** Local-first MVP complete, all P0-P3 priorities shipped
**Users:** Internal testing, preparing for beta
**Feedback:** Foundation proving solid - privacy-first architecture working, local-only data integrity maintained
**Codebase:**
- 89,472 lines of TypeScript/React
- React 19 + TypeScript 5.9 + Vite 7
- Zustand state management + IndexedDB persistence
- 128/128 tests passing (79 unit + 49 integration)
- Bundle size: 117KB gzip

**Known Issues:**
- Technical debt: IndexedDB query optimization, full-text search implementation
- UX polish needed across features
- Documentation sparse (user-facing docs minimal)
- Architecture decisions pending (block editor, graph database, local AI, CRDTs)

## v1.1+ Goals

**Vision:** Transform from solid foundation to competitive all-in-one platform while maintaining privacy-first differentiator.

**Motivation:**
- Foundation complete (P0-P3 100%) - ready to build vertically
- Quick wins available (tags, templates, celebrations) - high value, low effort
- Strategic features (collaboration, plugins, mobile) require architecture research
- Quality refinement needed before broad user adoption

**Scope (v1.1-v1.x):**
- Quality & Refinement: E2E tests, technical debt fixes, UX polish, documentation
- Quick Wins: Note tags, templates, task celebrations, natural language dates, markdown export
- P4 Planning: Research real-time collaboration, plugin system, mobile apps, publishing, automation
- Architecture Research: Block editor evaluation, graph database feasibility, local AI models, CRDT library selection

**Success Criteria:**
- [ ] All E2E critical flows covered by Playwright tests
- [ ] Technical debt addressed (IndexedDB optimization, full-text search, bundle analysis)
- [ ] User documentation complete (getting started, feature guides, troubleshooting)
- [ ] Quick wins shipped (5 features: tags, templates, celebrations, NL dates, markdown export)
- [ ] P4 architecture decisions documented with evidence (research findings, POCs, trade-off analysis)
- [ ] Platform ready for beta user onboarding

**Out of Scope (v1.1-v1.x):**
- Real-time collaboration implementation (research only)
- Plugin system implementation (research only)
- Mobile apps (research only)
- Publishing mode (research only)
- Advanced automation beyond current automation engine

---

<details>
<summary>Original Vision (v1.0 - Achieved)</summary>

**One-liner**: Privacy-first, local-only productivity platform replacing fragmented subscription stacks with unified, ethical tooling.

## Problem

Tools that organize our lives have become chaos themselves: scattered across subscriptions, duplicating effort, surrendering data, and chasing features over foundations. Users need a single, trustworthy platform that respects privacy, works offline, and puts the user in control.

## Success Criteria

How we knew it worked:

- [x] Local-first architecture with IndexedDB persistence - Achieved
- [x] Zero telemetry, no server data exfiltration - Achieved
- [x] E2EE backup/export system - Achieved
- [x] Notes, Tasks, Calendar, Time Tracking, Dashboard fully functional - Achieved
- [x] Widget system extensible and performant - Achieved
- [x] Type-safe throughout (TypeScript strict mode) - Achieved
- [x] Bundle size <120KB gzip - Achieved (117KB)
- [x] All P0-P3 features complete - Achieved (100%)

## Constraints

- Privacy-first: Local-first, no telemetry, E2EE backups/exports
- MIT/Apache/BSD licenses only (commercial viability)
- Build features custom, use utility libraries only (80%+ ownership)
- TypeScript strict mode throughout
- Tailwind semantic tokens (no hardcoded colors)
- Performance budgets: <100KB gz initial, <1s load, <100ms interaction

## Out of Scope (v1.0)

- Real-time collaboration
- Plugin ecosystem
- Mobile apps
- Publishing/sharing mode
- AI features beyond terminal integration
- Multi-user/team features
- Cloud sync (local-first only)

</details>

# Roadmap: Neuman Brain Post-P0-P3

## Overview

Transform Neuman Brain from solid foundation (P0-P3 complete) to competitive all-in-one platform through systematic quality refinement, quick-win features, strategic architecture research, and P4 planning. Four-phase approach optimizes for user value delivery (quality + quick wins first) while building knowledge base for future features (research + P4 planning).

## Milestones

- ✅ **v1.0 Foundation** - P0-P3 complete (shipped 2025-11-30)
- 🚧 **v1.1 Quality & Quick Wins** - Phases 1-2 (in progress)
- 📋 **v1.2 Architecture Foundation** - Phases 3-4 (planned)

## Phases

<details>
<summary>✅ v1.0 Foundation (P0-P3) - SHIPPED 2025-11-30</summary>

**Completed Work:**
- P0: Core features (Tasks, Notes, Calendar, Time Tracking, Dashboard)
- P1: Advanced features (Forms, Graph View, Daily Notes, Automation)
- P2: Polish (Gantt export, Recurrence UI, Tag enhancements, Analytics)
- P3: Advanced recurrence patterns

**Outcome:**
- 89,472 lines of TypeScript/React
- 128/128 tests passing
- 117KB gzip bundle size
- Local-first architecture proven
- Zero telemetry, E2EE backups working

</details>

### 🚧 v1.1 Quality & Quick Wins (In Progress)

**Milestone Goal:** Deliver production-ready platform with enhanced UX and high-value quick-win features.

#### Phase 1: Quality & Refinement
**Goal**: Harden platform for production with comprehensive testing, technical debt resolution, and user documentation
**Depends on**: v1.0 foundation complete
**Plans**: 3 plans (E2E tests, technical debt, documentation/UX)

Plans:
- [x] 01-01-PLAN.md: E2E Test Coverage - Playwright tests for all critical user flows (completed 2025-11-30)
- [x] 01-02-PLAN.md: Technical Debt Resolution - IndexedDB optimization, full-text search, bundle analysis (completed 2025-11-30)
- [ ] 01-03-PLAN.md: Documentation & UX Polish - User docs, onboarding, UI refinements

#### Phase 2: Quick Wins
**Goal**: Ship 5 high-value features that enhance daily usage without architectural complexity
**Depends on**: Phase 1 (quality foundation ensures quick wins don't break)
**Plans**: 5 plans (one per feature for surgical delivery)

Plans:
- [ ] 02-01-PLAN.md: Note Tags - Hashtag syntax, tag filtering, tag management UI
- [ ] 02-02-PLAN.md: Note Templates - Template creation, template library, template variables
- [ ] 02-03-PLAN.md: Task Completion Celebrations - Animations, sound effects, achievement tracking
- [ ] 02-04-PLAN.md: Natural Language Task Dates - Parser integration, smart date recognition
- [ ] 02-05-PLAN.md: Markdown Export - Notes/tasks to .md, preserve wiki links, folder structure

### 📋 v1.2 Architecture Foundation (Planned)

**Milestone Goal:** Establish architectural decisions and research findings to guide P4 feature development.

#### Phase 3: Architecture Research
**Goal**: Systematic evaluation of 4 architectural decisions with evidence, trade-offs, and recommendations
**Depends on**: Phase 2 (quick wins provide real usage data to inform architecture)
**Plans**: 4 plans (one per research domain)

Plans:
- [ ] 03-01-RESEARCH.md → 03-01-FINDINGS.md: Block Editor Evaluation - Notion/Obsidian approaches, library comparison
- [ ] 03-02-RESEARCH.md → 03-02-FINDINGS.md: Graph Database Feasibility - Backlinks/graph view requirements, library options
- [ ] 03-03-RESEARCH.md → 03-03-FINDINGS.md: Local AI Models - On-device processing feasibility, model selection
- [ ] 03-04-RESEARCH.md → 03-04-FINDINGS.md: CRDT Library Selection - Future collaboration requirements, library trade-offs

#### Phase 4: P4 Feature Planning
**Goal**: Detailed planning for 5 long-term features with architecture, milestones, and dependencies documented
**Depends on**: Phase 3 (research findings inform P4 plans)
**Plans**: 5 plans (one per P4 feature)

Plans:
- [ ] 04-01-PLAN.md: Real-time Collaboration Planning - Yjs integration, conflict resolution, sync architecture
- [ ] 04-02-PLAN.md: Plugin System Planning - API design, sandbox security, discovery/distribution
- [ ] 04-03-PLAN.md: Mobile Apps Planning - React Native vs native, sync strategy, offline-first
- [ ] 04-04-PLAN.md: Publishing Mode Planning - Static export, privacy controls, theming
- [ ] 04-05-PLAN.md: Advanced Automation Planning - Service Worker setup, trigger expansion, action library

## Progress

| Phase | Milestone | Plans Complete | Status | Completed |
|-------|-----------|----------------|--------|-----------|
| v1.0 Foundation | v1.0 | P0-P3 | Complete | 2025-11-30 |
| 1. Quality & Refinement | v1.1 | 2/3 | In progress | - |
| 2. Quick Wins | v1.1 | 0/5 | Not started | - |
| 3. Architecture Research | v1.2 | 0/4 | Not started | - |
| 4. P4 Feature Planning | v1.2 | 0/5 | Not started | - |

## Strategic Notes

**Dependency Rationale:**
- Phase 1 before Phase 2: Quality foundation prevents quick wins from introducing regressions
- Phase 2 before Phase 3: Real usage patterns from quick wins inform architecture research
- Phase 3 before Phase 4: Research findings directly guide P4 feature planning (e.g., CRDT choice affects collaboration plan)

**Value Delivery:**
- v1.1 (Phases 1-2): Immediate user value - production-ready platform + 5 new features
- v1.2 (Phases 3-4): Strategic value - informed decisions for long-term roadmap

**Execution Strategy:**
- Phases 1-2: Execute via `/run-plan` for surgical, focused delivery
- Phase 3: Use RESEARCH.md prompts → FINDINGS.md outputs for systematic evaluation
- Phase 4: Create detailed PLAN.md files for future execution (not immediate implementation)

# Getting Started with Neuman Brain

Welcome to Neuman Brain! This guide will help you get up and running with your privacy-first productivity platform.

## What is Neuman Brain?

Neuman Brain is a local-first productivity platform that combines notes, tasks, calendar, time tracking, and more - all in one place, all stored on your device. Your data never leaves your computer unless you choose to export it.

## First Time Setup

### 1. Access the Platform

Simply visit [https://brain.neuman.dev](https://brain.neuman.dev) in your web browser. No account creation required!

The platform works in any modern browser:
- Chrome/Edge (recommended for best performance)
- Firefox
- Safari
- Opera

### 2. Understanding Local Storage

All your data is stored in your browser's local storage. This means:
- Your information stays on your device
- No server uploads or downloads
- Complete privacy by default
- Works offline once loaded

**Important**: Your data is tied to your browser. If you clear browser data or uninstall your browser, you'll lose your information unless you've created backups.

### 3. The Interface Tour

When you first open Neuman Brain, you'll see:

**Left Sidebar**: Navigation to all major features
- Dashboard (home page with widgets)
- Notes (capture thoughts and ideas)
- Tasks (project and task management)
- Calendar (events and scheduling)
- Time Tracking (track your work hours)
- Forms (create and fill out custom forms)
- Diagrams (visual diagrams and flowcharts)
- Graph View (visualize connections between notes)
- Link Library (bookmark management)
- Automations (automate repetitive actions)
- Settings (customize your experience)

**Main Area**: Your active workspace (Dashboard, Notes editor, Kanban board, etc.)

**Top Right**: Quick actions and view controls

### 4. Start Creating Content

The best way to learn is by creating:

1. **Create your first note**: Click "Notes" in the sidebar, then "+ New Note"
2. **Add a task**: Click "Tasks" in the sidebar, then "+ Add Task" in any column
3. **Schedule an event**: Click "Calendar" and click any day to create an event
4. **Start tracking time**: Click the timer icon in the right sidebar

## Understanding Your Data

### Where Is My Data Stored?

Your data is stored in your browser's IndexedDB - a powerful local database that can hold 50GB+ of information. Think of it like a filing cabinet inside your browser.

### How Neuman Brain Respects Your Privacy

1. **No Account Required**: Start using immediately without creating an account
2. **No Cloud Sync**: Your data never touches our servers
3. **No Tracking**: No Google Analytics, no Facebook Pixel, no ads
4. **No Third Parties**: No data shared with anyone
5. **Open Source**: You can review the code yourself on GitHub

The only network requests made are:
- Loading the app itself (HTML, CSS, JavaScript)
- Optional widget data (weather, news, crypto prices) - only if you enable those widgets
- AI features - only if you configure them and provide your own API keys

## Backup Your Data (Critical!)

Since your data is stored locally, you must create backups to protect your work.

### Creating Your First Backup

1. Click **Settings** in the left sidebar
2. Navigate to **Backup & Sync**
3. Click **"Export Backup (.brain file)"**
4. Choose where to save the file
5. Store it somewhere safe (cloud storage, external drive, etc.)

Your backup file (`.brain` extension) contains everything:
- All notes and folders
- All tasks and projects
- All calendar events
- All time tracking entries
- All settings and preferences

### Automatic Backups (Chrome/Edge Only)

If you use Chrome or Edge, you can enable auto-save:

1. Go to **Settings > Backup & Sync**
2. Click **"Choose Auto-Save Folder"**
3. Select a folder (like Documents or a cloud-synced folder)
4. Enable **"Auto-Save on Changes"**

Neuman Brain will now automatically save a backup every time you make changes!

### How Often Should I Backup?

**Manual backups**: At least weekly, or before/after major work sessions

**Auto-save**: Enable it and forget about it (Chrome/Edge users)

### Restoring from Backup

If you need to restore your data:

1. Go to **Settings > Backup & Sync**
2. Click **"Import Backup (.brain file)"**
3. Select your backup file
4. Choose whether to merge with existing data or replace it
5. Click **Import**

## Exporting Data in Other Formats

You can export your data in various formats:

### Notes
- **Markdown**: Click the download icon next to any note
- **PDF**: Click the PDF icon next to any note

### Tasks
- **CSV**: Click "Export to CSV" in the Tasks page toolbar

### Calendar
- **ICS**: Click "Export ICS" in Calendar settings (compatible with Google Calendar, Outlook, etc.)

### Time Tracking
- **CSV**: Click "Export to CSV" in the Time Tracking page

## Common Questions

### Can I use Neuman Brain on multiple devices?

Yes, but you'll need to manually export/import backups to sync data between devices. There's no automatic cloud sync (by design, for privacy).

**Workflow**:
1. Work on Device A
2. Export backup
3. Save to cloud storage (Dropbox, Google Drive, etc.)
4. Download backup on Device B
5. Import backup

### What happens if I clear my browser data?

You'll lose all your Neuman Brain data unless you have a backup. Always keep backups!

### Can I use this on my phone?

Yes! The web app works on mobile browsers. For the best experience, you can "Add to Home Screen" to create a shortcut.

Note: Some features (like auto-save) may not work on mobile browsers.

### Is there a desktop app?

Not yet, but it's on the roadmap! For now, you can use it in any web browser.

### How much data can I store?

Your browser's IndexedDB typically allows 50GB+ of storage. That's enough for:
- Tens of thousands of notes
- Years of calendar events
- Decades of time tracking entries

## Next Steps

Now that you're set up, explore the detailed guides:

- [Notes Guide](./notes.md) - Learn about folders, search, and the rich text editor
- [Tasks Guide](./tasks.md) - Master the Kanban board and task management
- [Calendar Guide](./calendar.md) - Schedule events and manage your time
- [Time Tracking Guide](./time-tracking.md) - Track work hours and analyze productivity
- [Automation Guide](./automation.md) - Automate repetitive tasks

## Need Help?

- **Troubleshooting**: See the [Troubleshooting Guide](./troubleshooting.md)
- **Report Bugs**: [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues)
- **Feature Requests**: [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/discussions)
- **Email**: [brain@neuman.dev](mailto:brain@neuman.dev)

---

**Remember**: Create your first backup today! Go to Settings > Backup & Sync > Export Backup.

2. Click **"Import Backup (.brain file)"**
3. Select your backup file
4. Choose whether to merge with existing data or replace it
5. Click **Import**

## Exporting Data in Other Formats

You can export your data in various formats:

### Notes
- **Markdown**: Click the download icon next to any note
- **PDF**: Click the PDF icon next to any note

### Tasks
- **CSV**: Click "Export to CSV" in the Tasks page toolbar

### Calendar
- **ICS**: Click "Export ICS" in Calendar settings (compatible with Google Calendar, Outlook, etc.)

### Time Tracking
- **CSV**: Click "Export to CSV" in the Time Tracking page

## Common Questions

### Can I use Neuman Brain on multiple devices?

Yes, but you'll need to manually export/import backups to sync data between devices. There's no automatic cloud sync (by design, for privacy).

**Workflow**:
1. Work on Device A
2. Export backup
3. Save to cloud storage (Dropbox, Google Drive, etc.)
4. Download backup on Device B
5. Import backup

### What happens if I clear my browser data?

You'll lose all your Neuman Brain data unless you have a backup. Always keep backups!

### Can I use this on my phone?

Yes! The web app works on mobile browsers. For the best experience, you can "Add to Home Screen" to create a shortcut.

Note: Some features (like auto-save) may not work on mobile browsers.

### Is there a desktop app?

Not yet, but it's on the roadmap! For now, you can use it in any web browser.

### How much data can I store?

Your browser's IndexedDB typically allows 50GB+ of storage. That's enough for:
- Tens of thousands of notes
- Years of calendar events
- Decades of time tracking entries

## Next Steps

Now that you're set up, explore the detailed guides:

- [Notes Guide](./notes.md) - Learn about folders, search, and the rich text editor
- [Tasks Guide](./tasks.md) - Master the Kanban board and task management
- [Calendar Guide](./calendar.md) - Schedule events and manage your time
- [Time Tracking Guide](./time-tracking.md) - Track work hours and analyze productivity
- [Automation Guide](./automation.md) - Automate repetitive tasks

## Need Help?

- **Troubleshooting**: See the [Troubleshooting Guide](./troubleshooting.md)
- **Report Bugs**: [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/issues)
- **Feature Requests**: [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/travisjneuman/system.knowledgebase/discussions)
- **Email**: [brain@neuman.dev](mailto:brain@neuman.dev)

---

**Remember**: Create your first backup today! Go to Settings > Backup & Sync > Export Backup.

# Notes Guide

Your complete guide to capturing thoughts, ideas, and knowledge in Neuman Brain.

## Overview

The Notes feature is your personal knowledge base. Create unlimited notes, organize them in folders, link them together, and search across everything instantly.

## Creating Your First Note

1. Click **Notes** in the left sidebar
2. Click the **"+ New Note"** button in the top right
3. Your new note opens in the editor
4. Start typing to add content

**Tip**: Use the keyboard shortcut `Cmd+N` (Mac) or `Ctrl+N` (Windows) to create notes quickly!

## The Notes Interface

### Left Panel: Folders and Notes List

**Folders Section**:
- Browse your folder hierarchy
- Click any folder to view its notes
- Click "All Notes" to see everything
- Click "+ Folder" to create a new folder

**Search Box**:
- Type to search across all notes
- Use `Cmd+K` (Mac) or `Ctrl+K` (Windows) to focus the search
- Results appear instantly as you type

**Notes List**:
- Shows all notes in the current folder
- Click any note to open it
- Hover over notes to see action buttons

### Center Panel: Rich Text Editor

The editor is where you write and format your notes. You can use:
- Rich text formatting (bold, italic, headings, etc.)
- Markdown mode for plain text editing
- Slash commands for quick formatting
- Drag and drop images (coming soon)

### Right Panel: Note Actions

When a note is selected, you'll see:
- Last updated timestamp
- Word and character count
- Export options (Markdown, PDF)
- Pin/favorite controls

## Working with Folders

### Creating Folders

1. Click **"+ Folder"** in the Folders section
2. Enter a name for your folder
3. Press Enter or click outside to save

### Nested Folders

You can create folders inside folders:
1. Right-click any folder
2. Select "New Subfolder"
3. Name your subfolder

**Tip**: Use nested folders to organize by project, topic, or category.

Examples:
```
📁 Work
  📁 Projects
    📁 Website Redesign
    📁 Marketing Campaign
  📁 Meeting Notes
📁 Personal
  📁 Journal
  📁 Ideas
  📁 Learning
```

### Managing Folders

**Rename a folder**:
1. Hover over the folder name
2. Click the pencil icon
3. Type the new name
4. Press Enter to save

**Delete a folder**:
1. Hover over the folder name
2. Click the trash icon
3. Confirm deletion

**Warning**: Deleting a folder also deletes all notes inside it! Create a backup first if you're unsure.

**Move a note to a different folder**:
- Drag and drop is not yet supported
- As a workaround: Create a new note in the target folder and copy/paste the content

## Using the Rich Text Editor

### Basic Formatting

Use the toolbar or keyboard shortcuts:

| Format | Keyboard Shortcut | Toolbar Button |
|--------|------------------|----------------|
| Bold | `Cmd/Ctrl + B` | **B** |
| Italic | `Cmd/Ctrl + I` | *I* |
| Underline | `Cmd/Ctrl + U` | U |
| Code | `Cmd/Ctrl + E` | `</>` |
| Link | `Cmd/Ctrl + K` | 🔗 |

### Headings

Use headings to structure your notes:
- **Heading 1**: Main title (keyboard: `Cmd/Ctrl + Alt + 1`)
- **Heading 2**: Section title (keyboard: `Cmd/Ctrl + Alt + 2`)
- **Heading 3**: Subsection (keyboard: `Cmd/Ctrl + Alt + 3`)

### Lists

Create bullet lists or numbered lists:
- Click the list button in the toolbar
- Or use slash commands (see below)

### Slash Commands

Type `/` to see a menu of formatting options:

- `/heading` - Create a heading
- `/list` - Create a bullet list
- `/numbered` - Create a numbered list
- `/code` - Insert code block
- `/quote` - Insert blockquote
- `/table` - Insert table (coming soon)

### Markdown Mode

Prefer plain text? Switch to Markdown mode:
1. Click the "MD" button in the toolbar
2. Write in Markdown syntax
3. Click "Rich Text" to switch back

**Markdown basics**:
```markdown
# Heading 1
## Heading 2
### Heading 3

**bold text**
*italic text*
`inline code`

- Bullet point
- Another point

1. Numbered item
2. Another item

[Link text](https://example.com)
```

## Organizing Notes

### Pinning Notes

Pin important notes to keep them at the top:
1. Hover over a note in the list
2. Click the pin icon
3. Pinned notes stay at the top of the list

**Use cases**:
- Current project notes
- Reference materials you access frequently
- Templates for new notes

### Favoriting Notes

Mark notes as favorites for quick access:
1. Hover over a note in the list
2. Click the star icon
3. Filter by favorites using the filter menu

### Tags

Add tags to categorize notes across folders:
1. Select a note
2. Type `#` followed by a tag name in the note content
3. Tags appear in the note metadata
4. Click tags to filter notes

**Coming soon**: Dedicated tag management interface

### Filtering and Sorting

**Filter notes**:
- Click "All Notes" to see all notes
- Click a folder to see only that folder's notes
- Use the filter dropdown to show:
  - Favorites only
  - Pinned only
  - Archived notes

**Sort notes**:
- Click the sort dropdown to sort by:
  - Last updated (newest first)
  - Title (A-Z)
  - Created date

## Searching Notes

### Quick Search

1. Click the search box or press `Cmd/Ctrl + K`
2. Type your search query
3. Results appear instantly
4. Click any result to open that note

### Search Tips

- Search matches note titles and content
- Case-insensitive (finds "Task" and "task")
- Searches across all notes, regardless of folder
- Partial matches work ("meet" finds "meeting")

## Exporting Notes

### Export to Markdown

Download any note as a `.md` file:
1. Hover over a note in the list
2. Click the download icon
3. The file downloads to your Downloads folder

Use cases:
- Share notes with others
- Publish notes to a blog
- Version control with Git
- Import into other note apps (Obsidian, Notion, etc.)

### Export to PDF

Download any note as a PDF:
1. Hover over a note in the list
2. Click the PDF icon
3. The PDF downloads to your Downloads folder

Use cases:
- Print notes
- Share formatted notes
- Archive important documents

### Bulk Export

To export all notes:
1. Go to **Settings > Backup & Sync**
2. Click **"Export Backup (.brain file)"**
3. Your backup contains all notes in JSON format

To convert the backup to Markdown:
- Extract the notes using the import feature
- Export individual notes as Markdown

## Daily Notes (Optional Feature)

Daily notes create a new note automatically for each day.

### Enable Daily Notes

1. Go to **Settings > Daily Notes**
2. Toggle **"Enable Daily Notes"**
3. Choose a default folder for daily notes
4. Customize the note template (optional)

### Using Daily Notes

Once enabled:
- Click the calendar icon in the Notes page
- Click any date to create/open that day's note
- Or use the `?daily=true` URL parameter to open today's note

**Use cases**:
- Daily journal
- Work log
- Meeting notes
- Habit tracking

## Tips and Best Practices

### Naming Conventions

**Good note titles**:
- "Meeting Notes - Q1 Planning - 2025-01-15"
- "Project Ideas: Website Redesign"
- "How to Configure GitHub Actions"

**Avoid**:
- "Untitled" (always give your notes descriptive names)
- "Notes" (too generic)
- "Temp" (if it's temporary, why keep it?)

### Folder Structure Ideas

**By Project**:
```
📁 Project Apollo
📁 Project Nebula
📁 Archive
```

**By Type**:
```
📁 Meeting Notes
📁 Ideas
📁 Research
📁 Reference
```

**By Area of Life**:
```
📁 Work
📁 Personal
📁 Learning
📁 Health
```

**By Topic**:
```
📁 Programming
📁 Business
📁 Design
📁 Writing
```

### Regular Maintenance

**Weekly**:
- Review pinned notes (unpin completed items)
- File loose notes into folders
- Archive old notes you no longer need

**Monthly**:
- Review folder structure
- Export notes you want to keep forever
- Delete duplicate or outdated notes

## Advanced Features (Coming Soon)

The following features are planned:
- **Wiki Links**: Link notes together with `[[Note Title]]` syntax
- **Backlinks**: See which notes link to the current note
- **Graph View**: Visualize connections between notes
- **Templates**: Create note templates for recurring formats
- **Collaborative Editing**: Share notes with others
- **Version History**: See previous versions of a note

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Action | Windows/Linux | Mac |
|--------|---------------|-----|
| New note | `Ctrl + N` | `Cmd + N` |
| Save note | `Ctrl + S` | `Cmd + S` |
| Search | `Ctrl + K` | `Cmd + K` |
| Bold | `Ctrl + B` | `Cmd + B` |
| Italic | `Ctrl + I` | `Cmd + I` |
| Link | `Ctrl + K` | `Cmd + K` |
| Heading 1 | `Ctrl + Alt + 1` | `Cmd + Option + 1` |
| Heading 2 | `Ctrl + Alt + 2` | `Cmd + Option + 2` |
| Heading 3 | `Ctrl + Alt + 3` | `Cmd + Option + 3` |

## Troubleshooting

**Notes not saving?**
- Check your browser's storage settings
- Make sure you're not in private/incognito mode
- Try exporting a backup to verify data integrity

**Search not working?**
- The search updates as you type
- Make sure you're not filtering by a specific folder
- Try clearing the search and searching again

**Lost a note?**
- Check the "All Notes" view (might be in a different folder)
- Use search to find it by content
- If recently deleted, check if you have undo available

See the [Troubleshooting Guide](./troubleshooting.md) for more help.

## Next Steps

- **Organize**: Create a folder structure that makes sense for you
- **Capture**: Start writing notes daily to build the habit
- **Link**: Connect related notes with tags and references
- **Export**: Regularly export important notes as backups

Learn more:
- [Tasks Guide](./tasks.md) - Integrate tasks with your notes
- [Calendar Guide](./calendar.md) - Schedule time to work on note projects
- [Automation Guide](./automation.md) - Auto-create notes from events

# Tasks Guide

Master project and task management with Neuman Brain's Kanban board and timeline views.

## Overview

The Tasks feature helps you manage projects, track progress, and get things done. Use a visual Kanban board to move tasks through stages, create subtasks for complex work, and set dependencies to manage what needs to happen when.

## Getting Started with Tasks

1. Click **Tasks** in the left sidebar
2. You'll see a Kanban board with default columns:
   - **Backlog**: Tasks not yet started
   - **To Do**: Ready to work on
   - **In Progress**: Currently working on
   - **Done**: Completed tasks

## Creating Your First Task

### Quick Add

1. Click **"+ Add Task"** in any column
2. Type a task title
3. Press Enter

That's it! Your task is created.

### Detailed Task Creation

For more control:
1. Click the **"+"** icon in the top right of any column
2. A task detail modal opens
3. Fill in:
   - **Title**: What needs to be done (required)
   - **Description**: Additional details (optional)
   - **Due Date**: When it needs to be done (optional)
   - **Priority**: High, Medium, or Low (optional)
   - **Tags**: Categorize with labels (optional)

4. Click **"Create Task"**

## The Kanban Board

### Columns

Each column represents a stage in your workflow:
- **Backlog**: Ideas and future tasks
- **To Do**: Planned work
- **In Progress**: Active tasks
- **Done**: Completed work

### Moving Tasks

**Drag and Drop**:
1. Click and hold a task card
2. Drag it to a different column
3. Release to drop

**Keyboard Navigation** (coming soon):
- Use arrow keys to move between tasks
- Press Enter to open a task

### Customizing Columns

**Add a Column**:
1. Click **"+ Add Column"** on the right side
2. Enter a column name (e.g., "Review", "Testing", "Blocked")
3. Click Create

**Rename a Column**:
1. Click the pencil icon next to the column name
2. Type the new name
3. Press Enter

**Delete a Column**:
1. Click the trash icon next to the column name
2. Confirm deletion
3. Tasks in that column move to Backlog

**Reorder Columns**:
- Drag column headers left or right to reorder

### Custom Workflows

Create columns that match your process:

**Software Development**:
- Backlog → To Do → In Progress → Code Review → Testing → Done

**Content Creation**:
- Ideas → Research → Drafting → Editing → Published

**Support Tickets**:
- New → Assigned → In Progress → Waiting for Customer → Resolved

## Task Details

Click any task card to open the detail view.

### Basic Information

**Title**: The task name (click to edit)

**Description**: Detailed notes about the task
- Supports rich text formatting
- Add links, lists, and formatting
- Click "Edit" to modify

**Status**: The current column (click to change)

**Priority**: Importance level
- **High**: Red badge - urgent or critical
- **Medium**: Blue badge - normal priority
- **Low**: Gray badge - nice to have

### Due Dates

**Set a Due Date**:
1. Click the calendar icon
2. Select a date from the calendar
3. Click outside to save

**Clear a Due Date**:
- Click the "X" next to the date

**Overdue Indicator**:
- Overdue tasks show a red warning
- Approaching tasks (within 3 days) show a yellow warning

### Tags

Add tags to categorize and filter tasks:

1. Click **"+ Add Tag"**
2. Type a tag name (e.g., "urgent", "bug", "feature")
3. Press Enter

**Use Cases**:
- Project names (e.g., "website-redesign")
- Task types (e.g., "bug", "feature", "docs")
- Contexts (e.g., "home", "office", "errands")
- Priorities (e.g., "urgent", "low-priority")

**Filter by Tag**:
1. Click the filter icon in the toolbar
2. Select one or more tags
3. Only tasks with those tags appear

## Subtasks

Break complex tasks into smaller steps.

### Creating Subtasks

1. Open a task detail view
2. Scroll to the **Subtasks** section
3. Click **"+ Add Subtask"**
4. Enter a title
5. Press Enter

### Managing Subtasks

**Check off a subtask**: Click the checkbox when complete

**Edit a subtask**: Click the subtask to edit inline

**Delete a subtask**: Click the trash icon

**Progress Indicator**: The task card shows completion (e.g., "2/5 subtasks complete")

### Subtask Tips

**Good subtasks are**:
- Specific and actionable
- Small enough to complete in one sitting
- Ordered logically (first to last)

**Example - "Launch Website"**:
- [ ] Design homepage mockup
- [ ] Build homepage HTML/CSS
- [ ] Add contact form
- [ ] Set up hosting
- [ ] Deploy to production
- [ ] Test on mobile devices

## Task Dependencies

Manage which tasks must be completed before others can start.

### Creating Dependencies

1. Open a task detail view
2. Scroll to **Dependencies**
3. Click **"Add Dependency"**
4. Select a task that must be completed first
5. Click Save

### Dependency Types

**"This task is blocked by..."**:
- Another task must finish before this one can start
- Example: "Deploy to production" is blocked by "Pass QA testing"

**"This task blocks..."**:
- This task must finish before others can start
- Example: "Design mockup" blocks "Build homepage"

### Circular Dependency Protection

Neuman Brain prevents circular dependencies:
- Task A cannot block Task B if Task B already blocks Task A
- You'll see an error if you try to create a circular dependency

### Viewing Dependencies

**In Task Detail**:
- See all dependencies listed
- Click any dependency to jump to that task

**On Kanban Board**:
- Blocked tasks show a "blocked" indicator
- Hover to see what's blocking them

## Timeline View (Gantt Chart)

See tasks on a timeline to visualize project schedules.

### Switching to Timeline View

1. Click the **"Timeline"** tab at the top of the Tasks page
2. Your tasks appear on a horizontal timeline

### Reading the Timeline

**Horizontal Axis**: Time (days, weeks, or months)

**Vertical Axis**: Your tasks

**Task Bars**:
- Length = Duration (start to due date)
- Position = When the task occurs
- Color = Priority (red = high, blue = medium, gray = low)

**Dependencies**:
- Arrows connect dependent tasks
- Follow the arrows to see task order

### Timeline Controls

**Zoom**: Use the zoom buttons to see more/less time

**Scroll**: Drag the timeline left or right to see different time periods

**Filter**: Same filters work in both views (tags, status, etc.)

## Bulk Operations

Manage multiple tasks at once.

### Selecting Multiple Tasks

1. Click the **"Select"** button in the toolbar
2. Click checkboxes on task cards
3. Or click **"Select All"** to select all visible tasks

### Bulk Actions

**Change Status**:
1. Select tasks
2. Click **"Change Status"** in the toolbar
3. Choose a new status (column)
4. All selected tasks move

**Change Priority**:
1. Select tasks
2. Click **"Change Priority"**
3. Choose High, Medium, or Low

**Add Tags**:
1. Select tasks
2. Click **"Add Tag"**
3. Enter a tag name
4. Tag is added to all selected tasks

**Delete Tasks**:
1. Select tasks
2. Click **"Delete"** (trash icon)
3. Confirm deletion

**Warning**: Bulk delete is permanent! Export a backup first if unsure.

## Filtering and Searching

### Filter by Status

Click the filter icon and choose:
- **All Tasks**: Show everything
- **Active Only**: Hide completed tasks
- **Completed Only**: Show only done tasks
- **Overdue**: Show tasks past their due date

### Filter by Priority

Select priority levels to show:
- High priority only
- Medium priority only
- Low priority only
- Any combination

### Filter by Tags

Select one or more tags to show only tasks with those tags.

**AND logic**: Task must have ALL selected tags

**OR logic**: Use search instead (finds tasks with ANY tag)

### Search Tasks

Use the search box to find tasks by:
- Title
- Description content
- Tag names

Search is instant - results appear as you type.

## Task Recurrence (Coming Soon)

Create repeating tasks for recurring work.

**Planned features**:
- Daily, weekly, monthly recurrence
- Custom patterns (e.g., "every 3rd Monday")
- Automatic creation of next occurrence when completed

## Exporting Tasks

### Export to CSV

Download all tasks as a spreadsheet:
1. Click **"Export CSV"** in the toolbar
2. Open in Excel, Google Sheets, or any spreadsheet app

**CSV includes**:
- Task title and description
- Status, priority, tags
- Due date, created date
- Subtask count and completion

**Use cases**:
- Share tasks with non-Neuman-Brain users
- Analyze tasks in a spreadsheet
- Import into other project management tools
- Create reports for clients or managers

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Action | Windows/Linux | Mac |
|--------|---------------|-----|
| New task | `Ctrl + T` | `Cmd + T` |
| Search tasks | `Ctrl + F` | `Cmd + F` |
| Select multiple | `Ctrl + Click` | `Cmd + Click` |
| Open task detail | `Enter` | `Enter` |
| Close task detail | `Esc` | `Esc` |

## Tips and Best Practices

### Daily Task Management

**Morning**:
1. Review your "To Do" column
2. Move 2-3 tasks to "In Progress"
3. Check for overdue tasks

**During the day**:
- Update task status as you work
- Add notes to task descriptions
- Create new tasks as they come up

**End of day**:
- Move completed tasks to "Done"
- Review tomorrow's priorities
- Update due dates if needed

### Weekly Planning

**Weekly Review** (recommended):
1. Archive completed tasks (move to "Archive" column)
2. Groom the backlog (delete obsolete tasks)
3. Plan next week's work (move tasks to "To Do")
4. Set priorities and due dates

### Project Organization

**Use tags for projects**:
- Tag: "website-redesign"
- Tag: "Q1-marketing"
- Tag: "personal"

**Use columns for stages**:
- Backlog → Planning → Development → Review → Done

**Use subtasks for milestones**:
- Each major feature = 1 task
- Implementation steps = subtasks

### Priority Guidelines

**High Priority**:
- Urgent deadlines (today or tomorrow)
- Blockers for other tasks
- Critical bugs or issues

**Medium Priority**:
- Normal work
- This week's goals
- Important but not urgent

**Low Priority**:
- Nice to have
- Future ideas
- Low-impact improvements

## Integrations

### Calendar Integration

Tasks with due dates automatically appear in your Calendar view:
1. Open **Calendar**
2. Tasks show as events on their due dates
3. Click a task to open it

### Time Tracking Integration

Track time spent on tasks:
1. Open **Time Tracking**
2. Start a timer
3. Select a task from the dropdown
4. Time is logged to that task

## Troubleshooting

**Tasks not saving?**
- Check browser storage settings
- Make sure you're not in private/incognito mode
- Export a backup to verify data

**Drag and drop not working?**
- Make sure you're clicking and holding the task card
- Some browsers require a slight drag before detecting movement
- Try clicking the status dropdown instead

**Dependencies not working?**
- Check for circular dependencies (A blocks B, B blocks A)
- Make sure both tasks exist
- Try removing and re-adding the dependency

See the [Troubleshooting Guide](./troubleshooting.md) for more help.

## Next Steps

- **Create**: Set up your first project with tasks
- **Organize**: Customize columns for your workflow
- **Track**: Use subtasks and dependencies for complex projects
- **Review**: Schedule weekly reviews to stay on top of work

Learn more:
- [Notes Guide](./notes.md) - Take notes about your tasks
- [Calendar Guide](./calendar.md) - Schedule time to work on tasks
- [Time Tracking Guide](./time-tracking.md) - Track time spent on tasks
- [Automation Guide](./automation.md) - Automate task creation and updates

Master project and task management with Neuman Brain's Kanban board and timeline views.

## Overview

The Tasks feature helps you manage projects, track progress, and get things done. Use a visual Kanban board to move tasks through stages, create subtasks for complex work, and set dependencies to manage what needs to happen when.

## Getting Started with Tasks

1. Click **Tasks** in the left sidebar
2. You'll see a Kanban board with default columns:

   - **To Do**: Ready to work on
   - **In Progress**: Currently working on
   - **Done**: Completed tasks

## Creating Your First Task

1. Click **"+ Add Task"** in any column
2. Type a task title
3. Press Enter

That's it! Your task is created.

### Detailed Task Creation

For more control:
1. Click the **"+"** icon in the top right of any column
2. A task detail modal opens
3. Fill in:
   - **Title**: What needs to be done (required)
   - **Description**: Additional details (optional)
   - **Due Date**: When it needs to be done (optional)
   - **Priority**: High, Medium, or Low (optional)
   - **Tags**: Categorize with labels (optional)

4. Click **"Create Task"**

- **To Do**: Planned work
- **In Progress**: Active tasks

### Custom Workflows

### Subtask Tips

input.content:
# Calendar Guide

Your complete guide to scheduling events and managing time in Neuman Brain.

## Overview

The Calendar feature helps you schedule events, track appointments, and visualize your time. Create one-time or recurring events, import from other calendars, and integrate with your tasks and time tracking.

## Getting Started

1. Click **Calendar** in the left sidebar
2. You'll see the current month by default
3. Click any day to create an event

## Creating Events

### Quick Event Creation

1. Click any date on the calendar
2. A "Day Detail" modal opens
3. Click **"+ Create Event"**
4. Enter an event title
5. Click **Create**

The event is created with these defaults:
- Start time: Current time (or 9:00 AM if in the past)
- Duration: 1 hour
- No recurrence
- No reminders

### Detailed Event Creation

For full control:
1. Click a date on the calendar
2. Click **"+ Create Event"**
3. Fill in all fields:
   - **Title**: Event name (required)
   - **Description**: Additional details (optional)
   - **Start Date/Time**: When it begins
   - **End Date/Time**: When it ends
   - **Location**: Where it happens (optional)
   - **Recurrence**: Repeat pattern (optional)
   - **Reminders**: Notification timing (optional)

4. Click **"Save"**

## Calendar Views

Switch between different views to see your schedule:

### Month View (Default)

**Shows**: Full month grid with events on each day

**Best for**:
- Overview of the month
- Finding free days
- Planning ahead

**Controls**:
- Click ◀ ▶ arrows to navigate months
- Click "Today" to jump to current month
- Click any day to see that day's events in detail

### Week View

**Shows**: 7 days with hourly time slots

**Best for**:
- Detailed weekly planning
- Seeing time conflicts
- Scheduling appointments

**Controls**:
- Scroll vertically to see different times
- Click any time slot to create an event
- Drag events to reschedule

### Day View

**Shows**: Single day with hourly breakdown

**Best for**:
- Today's schedule
- Dense schedules with many events
- Hourly planning

**Controls**:
- Use ◀ ▶ to navigate days
- Click time slots to create events

### Agenda View

**Shows**: List of upcoming events (next 30 days)

**Best for**:
- Quick overview of what's coming
- Reviewing all events chronologically
- Finding a specific event

**Controls**:
- Scroll to see more events
- Click any event to edit
- Filter by type or tag

## Day Detail Modal

Click any calendar day to see a detailed view of that date.

### Features

**Events for that day**:
- See all events scheduled
- Click any event to edit
- Create new events

**Tasks due that day**:
- See tasks with this due date
- Click to open task details
- Mark tasks complete

**Time tracking entries**:
- See work logged on this day
- Total hours tracked
- Click to see details

**Quick actions**:
- Create event
- Create task with this due date
- Start time tracking for today

## Event Details

Click any event to open the detail view.

### Basic Information

**Title**: The event name

**Description**: Additional notes, agenda, links, etc.
- Supports plain text
- Add meeting links (Zoom, Google Meet)
- Paste in agendas or talking points

**Date/Time**:
- Start date and time
- End date and time
- All-day event toggle

**Location**: Where the event takes place
- Physical address
- Room number
- Video call link

### Event Colors

Each event can have a color for visual organization:
- **Blue**: Work/Professional
- **Green**: Personal
- **Red**: Urgent/Important
- **Purple**: Social/Fun
- **Orange**: Health/Fitness
- **Gray**: Other

**To change color**:
1. Open event details
2. Click the color dropdown
3. Select a new color

### Multi-Day Events

Create events that span multiple days:
1. Set start date (e.g., Monday)
2. Set end date (e.g., Friday)
3. Toggle "All-day event" if appropriate

**Examples**:
- Vacation (5-day trip)
- Conference (3-day event)
- Project sprint (2-week period)

**Display**: Multi-day events show as a banner across the calendar.

## Recurring Events

Create events that repeat on a schedule.

### Setting Up Recurrence

1. Open event details
2. Click **"Recurrence"** section
3. Choose a pattern:
   - **Daily**: Every day
   - **Weekly**: Every week on the same day(s)
   - **Monthly**: Same date each month
   - **Yearly**: Annual event

4. Set options:
   - **Repeat every**: Interval (e.g., every 2 weeks)
   - **Ends**: Never, on a date, or after X occurrences

### Examples

**Daily standup meeting**:
- Recurrence: Daily
- Repeat every: 1 day
- Monday-Friday only
- Ends: Never

**Monthly team meeting**:
- Recurrence: Monthly
- Repeat every: 1 month
- On: First Monday
- Ends: Never

**Weekly review**:
- Recurrence: Weekly
- Repeat every: 1 week
- On: Friday
- Ends: Never

**Annual birthday**:
- Recurrence: Yearly
- Repeat every: 1 year
- Ends: Never

### Editing Recurring Events

When you edit a recurring event, you'll see options:
- **This event only**: Change just this occurrence
- **This and future events**: Change from this point forward
- **All events**: Change the entire series

### Deleting Recurring Events

Same options as editing:
- Delete just this occurrence
- Delete this and all future occurrences
- Delete the entire series

## Reminders and Notifications

Get notified before events start.

### Setting Reminders

1. Open event details
2. Click **"+ Add Reminder"**
3. Choose timing:
   - 5 minutes before
   - 15 minutes before
   - 30 minutes before
   - 1 hour before
   - 1 day before
   - Custom time

4. Click Save

### Multiple Reminders

You can add multiple reminders to one event:
- Example: 1 day before (email reminder) + 15 minutes before (final reminder)

### Browser Notifications

Neuman Brain uses your browser's notification system:
- You'll see a desktop notification
- With the event title and time
- Click to open the event

**Enable notifications**:
1. Your browser will prompt for permission
2. Click "Allow" when asked
3. Notifications will work from then on

**Not seeing notifications?**
- Check browser notification settings
- Make sure Neuman Brain tab is open
- Check Do Not Disturb settings on your computer

## Importing and Exporting

### Importing Calendar Files (ICS)

Import events from Google Calendar, Outlook, Apple Calendar, etc.

1. Export an `.ics` file from your other calendar
2. In Neuman Brain, click **Calendar**
3. Click **"Import"** in the toolbar
4. Select your `.ics` file
5. Choose options:
   - Replace existing events
   - Merge with existing events
6. Click **Import**

**Supported formats**: ICS (iCalendar standard)

**What imports**:
- Event titles and descriptions
- Start and end times
- Locations
- Recurrence patterns
- Reminders (converted to nearest Neuman Brain option)

**What doesn't import**:
- Attendees (not yet supported)
- Attachments (not yet supported)
- Video call links (import as plain text in description)

### Exporting Calendar

Export your calendar to use in other apps:

1. Click **"Export"** in the toolbar
2. Choose date range:
   - This month
   - This year
   - All events
   - Custom range
3. Click **"Export as ICS"**
4. File downloads to your Downloads folder

**Use cases**:
- Backup your calendar
- Share your schedule with others
- Import into Google Calendar, Outlook, etc.
- Print a physical calendar

## Filtering Events

View only certain types of events:

**By Tag**:
- Tag events (e.g., "work", "personal", "family")
- Use filter to show only selected tags

**By Date Range**:
- Show only upcoming events
- Show only past events
- Custom date range

**By Status**:
- Confirmed events
- Tentative events
- Canceled events (if implemented)

## Conflict Detection

Neuman Brain warns you about scheduling conflicts.

**When you see a conflict warning**:
1. An event overlaps with another event
2. You'll see a yellow warning badge
3. Click to see which events conflict

**Resolving conflicts**:
- Reschedule one event
- Shorten event duration
- Mark one event as "tentative"
- Ignore if intentional (e.g., backup plans)

## Time Blocking

Use events to block time for focused work.

### Creating Time Blocks

1. Create an event for work time
2. Title: What you'll work on (e.g., "Writing time", "Email processing")
3. Set duration (30 min, 1 hour, 2 hours)
4. Repeat if recurring (daily writing time, weekly planning)

### Time Block Examples

**Deep work blocks**:
- 9:00 AM - 11:00 AM: Focus work (no meetings)
- Recurring: Daily

**Admin time**:
- 2:00 PM - 3:00 PM: Email and messages
- Recurring: Daily

**Planning time**:
- Friday 4:00 PM: Weekly review
- Recurring: Weekly

### Color-Coding Time Blocks

Use colors to distinguish block types:
- **Blue**: Focus work
- **Green**: Admin/maintenance
- **Purple**: Learning/development
- **Orange**: Exercise/breaks

## Integration with Other Features

### Tasks Integration

- Shows task title on due date
- Click to open task details
- Complete tasks from calendar view

**To see tasks on calendar**:
- Tasks automatically appear
- Toggle "Show Tasks" if you want to hide them

### Time Tracking Integration

1. Event is happening
2. Open Time Tracking
3. Start timer
4. Select the event from dropdown
5. Time is logged and associated with the event

## Tips and Best Practices

### Color-Coding Strategy

**By Type**:
- Blue: Work meetings
- Green: Personal appointments
- Red: Urgent/critical events
- Purple: Social plans

**By Project**:
- Assign each project a color
- All project events use that color
- Easy to see project distribution

**By Energy Level**:
- Red: High-energy tasks (requires focus)
- Orange: Medium-energy (normal work)
- Green: Low-energy (routine tasks)

### Weekly Planning

**Sunday evening or Monday morning**:
1. Review the week ahead in Week View
2. Add any new events
3. Time-block focus work
4. Check for conflicts
5. Set reminders for important events

### Monthly Review

**End of month**:
1. Switch to Month View
2. Review what happened
3. Look ahead to next month
4. Schedule recurring events
5. Export a backup

### Sharing Your Calendar

**Without account sharing**:
1. Export your calendar as ICS
2. Share the ICS file
3. Others can import into their calendar app
4. Update: Export and share again

**Future feature**: Real-time calendar sharing (coming in collaboration phase)

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Action | Windows/Linux | Mac |
|--------|---------------|-----|
| New event | `Ctrl + E` | `Cmd + E` |
| Today | `T` | `T` |

# Calendar Guide

Your complete guide to scheduling events and managing time in Neuman Brain.

## Overview

The Calendar feature helps you schedule events, track appointments, and visualize your time. Create one-time or recurring events, import from other calendars, and integrate with your tasks and time tracking.

## Getting Started

1. Click **Calendar** in the left sidebar
2. You'll see the current month by default
3. Click any day to create an event

## Creating Events

### Quick Event Creation

1. Click any date on the calendar
2. A "Day Detail" modal opens
3. Click **"+ Create Event"**
4. Enter an event title
5. Click **Create**

The event is created with these defaults:
- Start time: Current time (or 9:00 AM if in the past)
- Duration: 1 hour
- No recurrence
- No reminders

### Detailed Event Creation

For full control:
1. Click a date on the calendar
2. Click **"+ Create Event"**
3. Fill in all fields:
   - **Title**: Event name (required)
   - **Description**: Additional details (optional)
   - **Start Date/Time**: When it begins
   - **End Date/Time**: When it ends
   - **Location**: Where it happens (optional)
   - **Recurrence**: Repeat pattern (optional)
   - **Reminders**: Notification timing (optional)

4. Click **"Save"**

## Calendar Views

Switch between different views to see your schedule:

### Month View (Default)

**Shows**: Full month grid with events on each day

**Best for**:
- Overview of the month
- Finding free days
- Planning ahead

**Controls**:
- Click ◀ ▶ arrows to navigate months
- Click "Today" to jump to current month
- Click any day to see that day's events in detail

### Week View

**Shows**: 7 days with hourly time slots

**Best for**:
- Detailed weekly planning
- Seeing time conflicts
- Scheduling appointments

**Controls**:
- Scroll vertically to see different times
- Click any time slot to create an event
- Drag events to reschedule

### Day View

**Shows**: Single day with hourly breakdown

**Best for**:
- Today's schedule
- Dense schedules with many events
- Hourly planning

**Controls**:
- Use ◀ ▶ to navigate days
- Click time slots to create events

### Agenda View

**Shows**: List of upcoming events (next 30 days)

**Best for**:
- Quick overview of what's coming
- Reviewing all events chronologically
- Finding a specific event

**Controls**:
- Scroll to see more events
- Click any event to edit
- Filter by type or tag

## Day Detail Modal

Click any calendar day to see a detailed view of that date.

### Features

**Events for that day**:
- See all events scheduled
- Click any event to edit
- Create new events

**Tasks due that day**:
- See tasks with this due date
- Click to open task details
- Mark tasks complete

**Time tracking entries**:
- See work logged on this day
- Total hours tracked
- Click to see details

**Quick actions**:
- Create event
- Create task with this due date
- Start time tracking for today

## Event Details

Click any event to open the detail view.

### Basic Information

**Title**: The event name

**Description**: Additional notes, agenda, links, etc.
- Supports plain text
- Add meeting links (Zoom, Google Meet)
- Paste in agendas or talking points

**Date/Time**:
- Start date and time
- End date and time
- All-day event toggle

**Location**: Where the event takes place
- Physical address
- Room number
- Video call link

### Event Colors

Each event can have a color for visual organization:
- **Blue**: Work/Professional
- **Green**: Personal
- **Red**: Urgent/Important
- **Purple**: Social/Fun
- **Orange**: Health/Fitness
- **Gray**: Other

**To change color**:
1. Open event details
2. Click the color dropdown
3. Select a new color

### Multi-Day Events

Create events that span multiple days:
1. Set start date (e.g., Monday)
2. Set end date (e.g., Friday)
3. Toggle "All-day event" if appropriate

**Examples**:
- Vacation (5-day trip)
- Conference (3-day event)
- Project sprint (2-week period)

**Display**: Multi-day events show as a banner across the calendar.

## Recurring Events

Create events that repeat on a schedule.

### Setting Up Recurrence

1. Open event details
2. Click **"Recurrence"** section
3. Choose a pattern:
   - **Daily**: Every day
   - **Weekly**: Every week on the same day(s)
   - **Monthly**: Same date each month
   - **Yearly**: Annual event

4. Set options:
   - **Repeat every**: Interval (e.g., every 2 weeks)
   - **Ends**: Never, on a date, or after X occurrences

### Examples

**Daily standup meeting**:
- Recurrence: Daily
- Repeat every: 1 day
- Monday-Friday only
- Ends: Never

**Monthly team meeting**:
- Recurrence: Monthly
- Repeat every: 1 month
- On: First Monday
- Ends: Never

**Weekly review**:
- Recurrence: Weekly
- Repeat every: 1 week
- On: Friday
- Ends: Never

**Annual birthday**:
- Recurrence: Yearly
- Repeat every: 1 year
- Ends: Never

### Editing Recurring Events

When you edit a recurring event, you'll see options:
- **This event only**: Change just this occurrence
- **This and future events**: Change from this point forward
- **All events**: Change the entire series

### Deleting Recurring Events

Same options as editing:
- Delete just this occurrence
- Delete this and all future occurrences
- Delete the entire series

## Reminders and Notifications

Get notified before events start.

### Setting Reminders

1. Open event details
2. Click **"+ Add Reminder"**
3. Choose timing:
   - 5 minutes before
   - 15 minutes before
   - 30 minutes before
   - 1 hour before
   - 1 day before
   - Custom time

4. Click Save

### Multiple Reminders

You can add multiple reminders to one event:
- Example: 1 day before (email reminder) + 15 minutes before (final reminder)

### Browser Notifications

Neuman Brain uses your browser's notification system:
- You'll see a desktop notification
- With the event title and time
- Click to open the event

**Enable notifications**:
1. Your browser will prompt for permission
2. Click "Allow" when asked
3. Notifications will work from then on

**Not seeing notifications?**
- Check browser notification settings
- Make sure Neuman Brain tab is open
- Check Do Not Disturb settings on your computer

## Importing and Exporting

### Importing Calendar Files (ICS)

Import events from Google Calendar, Outlook, Apple Calendar, etc.

1. Export an `.ics` file from your other calendar
2. In Neuman Brain, click **Calendar**
3. Click **"Import"** in the toolbar
4. Select your `.ics` file
5. Choose options:
   - Replace existing events
   - Merge with existing events
6. Click **Import**

**Supported formats**: ICS (iCalendar standard)

**What imports**:
- Event titles and descriptions
- Start and end times
- Locations
- Recurrence patterns
- Reminders (converted to nearest Neuman Brain option)

**What doesn't import**:
- Attendees (not yet supported)
- Attachments (not yet supported)
- Video call links (import as plain text in description)

### Exporting Calendar

Export your calendar to use in other apps:

1. Click **"Export"** in the toolbar
2. Choose date range:
   - This month
   - This year
   - All events
   - Custom range
3. Click **"Export as ICS"**
4. File downloads to your Downloads folder

**Use cases**:
- Backup your calendar
- Share your schedule with others
- Import into Google Calendar, Outlook, etc.
- Print a physical calendar

## Filtering Events

View only certain types of events:

**By Tag**:
- Tag events (e.g., "work", "personal", "family")
- Use filter to show only selected tags

**By Date Range**:
- Show only upcoming events
- Show only past events
- Custom date range

**By Status**:
- Confirmed events
- Tentative events
- Canceled events (if implemented)

## Conflict Detection

Neuman Brain warns you about scheduling conflicts.

**When you see a conflict warning**:
1. An event overlaps with another event
2. You'll see a yellow warning badge
3. Click to see which events conflict

**Resolving conflicts**:
- Reschedule one event
- Shorten event duration
- Mark one event as "tentative"
- Ignore if intentional (e.g., backup plans)

## Time Blocking

Use events to block time for focused work.

### Creating Time Blocks

1. Create an event for work time
2. Title: What you'll work on (e.g., "Writing time", "Email processing")
3. Set duration (30 min, 1 hour, 2 hours)
4. Repeat if recurring (daily writing time, weekly planning)

### Time Block Examples

**Deep work blocks**:
- 9:00 AM - 11:00 AM: Focus work (no meetings)
- Recurring: Daily

**Admin time**:
- 2:00 PM - 3:00 PM: Email and messages
- Recurring: Daily

**Planning time**:
- Friday 4:00 PM: Weekly review

### Color-Coding Time Blocks

- **Purple**: Learning/development

input.content:
# Time Tracking Guide

Track your work hours, analyze productivity, and understand where your time goes.

## Overview

The Time Tracking feature helps you log hours worked, track project time, and generate reports. Use the built-in timer for real-time tracking or add manual entries for retroactive logging.

## Getting Started

Time tracking is available in two places:

1. **Sidebar Timer** (always visible)
   - Quick start/stop timer on the right side of every page
   - Floating panel for ongoing tracking
   - Minimal interface for maximum convenience

2. **Timer Tab** (Schedule page)
   - Full-featured tracking interface
   - Weekly statistics and charts
   - Recent entries with one-click continue
   - Today's total and daily average

## The Sidebar Timer

### Starting a Timer

1. Look for the timer panel on the right side of the screen
2. (Optional) Select a project from the dropdown
3. (Optional) Add a description
4. Click the **Play** button (▶)
5. Timer starts counting

### Stopping a Timer

1. Click the **Stop** button (■)
2. Timer saves automatically
3. Entry appears in your time tracking history

### Pausing and Resuming

**Pause**:
- Click the **Pause** button (❚❚)
- Timer stops counting but doesn't save
- Use when taking a short break

**Resume**:
- Click the **Play** button (▶) again
- Timer continues from where it left off

### What Gets Tracked

Each time entry records:
- **Start time**: When you clicked Play
- **End time**: When you clicked Stop
- **Duration**: Total time elapsed
- **Project**: Which project (if selected)
- **Description**: What you worked on
- **Date**: Automatically set to today

## The Timer Tab

Access the full time tracking interface:

1. Click **Schedule** in the sidebar
2. Click the **Timer** tab at the top

### Timer Tab Features

**Active Timer Section**:
- Same timer controls as the sidebar
- Larger, more visible interface
- Project and description fields

**Weekly Statistics**:
- Visual chart showing daily hours
- Total hours this week
- Today's total
- Daily average for the week

**Recent Entries** (48-hour history):
- See your last few time entries
- Click any entry to continue tracking the same task
- One-click to resume work on recent projects

## Creating Manual Entries

Add time retroactively when you forgot to start the timer.

### Adding a Manual Entry

1. Go to **Time Tracking** page (or Timer tab)
2. Click **"+ Add Entry"**
3. Fill in details:
   - **Date**: When you worked
   - **Start Time**: When you started
   - **End Time**: When you finished
   - **Project**: Which project (optional)
   - **Description**: What you worked on (optional)
4. Click **Save**

**Calculated automatically**: Duration is calculated from start/end times.

### Editing Entries

1. Find the entry in your list
2. Click the **Edit** button (pencil icon)
3. Change any fields
4. Click **Save**

### Deleting Entries

1. Find the entry in your list
2. Click the **Delete** button (trash icon)
3. Confirm deletion

**Warning**: Deletion is permanent! Export your data first if unsure.

## Projects

Organize your time entries by project.

### Creating Projects

1. In the sidebar timer or Timer tab
2. Click the project dropdown
3. Type a new project name
4. Press Enter

Project is created and selected automatically.

### Project Examples

**By Client**:
- "Acme Corp"
- "Smith Industries"
- "Personal"

**By Type of Work**:
- "Development"
- "Design"
- "Meetings"
- "Admin"

**By Product**:
- "Website Redesign"
- "Mobile App"
- "Marketing Campaign"

### Managing Projects

**Rename a project**:
- Go to Time Tracking page
- Click **"Manage Projects"**
- Edit project name
- Save

**Delete a project**:
- Click **"Manage Projects"**
- Click trash icon next to project
- Confirm deletion

**Warning**: Deleting a project doesn't delete time entries - they just become "Unassigned".

### Project Colors (Coming Soon)

Color-code projects for visual organization in reports and calendars.

## Viewing Time Entries

### Time Tracking Page Views

Switch between different views to analyze your time:

**Daily View**:
- See entries for a specific day
- Total hours for that day
- Grouped by project

**Weekly View**:
- 7 days of time entries
- Total hours per day
- Total for the week

**Monthly View**:
- Calendar heat map showing activity
- Total hours for each day
- Monthly total
- Project breakdown

### Monthly Calendar View

Visual representation of your tracked time:

**Heat Map**:
- Darker colors = more hours tracked
- Lighter colors = fewer hours
- White/gray = no time tracked

**Daily Totals**:
- Hover over any day to see hours
- Click day for detailed view

**Project Colors**:
- Each project has a color
- Days show project distribution

**Use cases**:
- Spot gaps in tracking
- See work patterns (busy vs. slow days)
- Identify most active periods

## Reports and Analytics

Analyze your time tracking data.

### Weekly Statistics

See how your week breaks down:
- **Total Hours**: Sum of all tracked time this week
- **Daily Average**: Total ÷ 7 days
- **Today's Total**: Hours tracked today
- **Chart**: Visual breakdown by day

### Monthly Reports

Comprehensive monthly view:
- **Total Hours**: Sum for the entire month
- **Billable Hours**: (Coming soon) Hours marked as billable
- **Project Breakdown**: Pie chart or table showing time per project
- **Daily Average**: Total hours ÷ days in month

### Exporting Reports

Download your time data:

1. Click **"Export"** in the Time Tracking page
2. Choose format:
   - **CSV**: Open in Excel, Google Sheets
   - **PDF**: (Coming soon) Formatted report
3. Choose date range:
   - This week
   - This month
   - Last month
   - Custom range
4. Click **Export**

**CSV includes**:
- Date and time
- Duration (in hours)
- Project name
- Description
- Start and end times

**Use cases**:
- Client invoicing
- Payroll submission
- Productivity analysis
- Tax records

## Continuing Previous Work

Quickly resume tracking on tasks you've worked on before.

### Recent Entries (48 Hours)

**In Timer Tab**:
- See your last few time entries
- Click **"Continue"** on any entry
- Timer starts with the same project and description
- Perfect for returning to work after a break

**Why 48 hours?**
- Focuses on recent, relevant work
- Keeps the list manageable
- Older entries still accessible in main Time Tracking page

### One-Click Resume

The fastest way to start tracking:
1. Open Timer tab
2. Find the task in Recent Entries
3. Click **"Continue"**
4. Timer starts immediately

**No need to**:
- Re-select the project
- Re-type the description
- Remember what you were working on

## Tips and Best Practices

### When to Use the Timer

**Best for**:
- Focused work sessions
- Client work that's billed hourly
- Understanding how long tasks actually take
- Improving time estimates

**Not necessary for**:
- Very short tasks (< 5 minutes)
- Breaks and lunch
- Personal time (unless tracking for yourself)

### Building the Habit

**Start simple**:
- Week 1: Just start/stop the timer, no projects
- Week 2: Add projects
- Week 3: Add descriptions
- Week 4: Review your weekly data

**Use triggers**:
- "When I start work" → start timer
- "When I finish a task" → stop timer
- "End of day" → review entries

**Be consistent, not perfect**:
- Missing an hour? Estimate and log it manually
- Forgot to stop the timer? Edit the entry
- Tracking 80% of your time is better than 0%

### Accurate Time Tracking

**Do**:
- Start the timer when you actually begin work
- Stop when you take breaks (or track breaks separately)
- Add descriptions while you remember them
- Review entries daily for accuracy

**Don't**:
- Round excessively (defeats the purpose)
- Forget breaks (inflates hours)
- Track non-work time as work
- Estimate if you can track instead

### Weekly Review

Every Friday (or Monday):
1. Review total hours tracked
2. Check for missing entries
3. Ensure all entries have projects
4. Export report if needed for invoicing

### Privacy Note

All time tracking data stays on your device:
- Not sent to any server
- Not visible to anyone else
- Completely private
- Export and backup at will

## Billable Hours (Coming Soon)

Track which hours to invoice clients.

**Planned features**:
- Mark entries as billable/non-billable
- Set hourly rates per project
- Generate invoice reports
- Calculate total billable amount

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Action | Windows/Linux | Mac |
|--------|---------------|-----|
| Start/Stop Timer | `Ctrl + Shift + T` | `Cmd + Shift + T` |
| New Manual Entry | `Ctrl + Shift + E` | `Cmd + Shift + E` |

## Troubleshooting

**Timer not starting?**
- Check if another timer is already running
- Try refreshing the page
- Check browser console for errors

**Entries not saving?**
- Check browser storage settings
- Make sure you're not in private/incognito mode
- Export a backup to verify data

**Lost time entry?**
- Check if you're viewing the right date range
- Try searching by project or description
- Check the full Time Tracking page (not just recent entries)

**Timer stuck on 0:00?**
- Click Stop and start again
- Refresh the page
- If you logged time, add it manually

**Weekly stats showing wrong total?**
- Make sure you're viewing the correct week
- Check for duplicate entries (delete duplicates)
- Verify start/end times are correct

See the [Troubleshooting Guide](./troubleshooting.md) for more help.

## Advanced Features (Coming Soon)

**Planned features**:
- Billable vs. non-billable tracking
- Hourly rates and automatic invoicing
- Time estimates vs. actuals
- Pomodoro timer integration
- Idle time detection
- Team time tracking (collaboration phase)

## Integration with Other Features

### Tasks Integration

Track time on specific tasks:
1. Start timer
2. Select task from dropdown
3. Time logged to that task
4. See total time per task in Tasks page

### Calendar Integration

Time entries can link to calendar events:
1. Event is happening
2. Start timer
3. Select the event
4. Time tracked and associated with event

### Automation Integration

Automate time tracking:
- Auto-start timer when task moves to "In Progress"
- Auto-stop timer when task marked Done
- Create time entries from calendar events
- (See [Automation Guide](./automation.md))

## Next Steps

- **Start Tracking**: Begin with simple start/stop today
- **Add Projects**: Organize entries next week
- **Review**: Check weekly stats every Friday
- **Export**: Download monthly reports for records

Learn more:
- [Tasks Guide](./tasks.md) - Track time on tasks
- [Calendar Guide](./calendar.md) - Schedule focus time
- [Automation Guide](./automation.md) - Automate timer workflows
- [Getting Started](./getting-started.md) - Backup your time data
